description_long: This is a sleek, modern airplane with a sharply angular, swept-wing design and a streamlined fuselage. The wings are mid-mounted and sharply swept back, with two large jet engines mounted at the rear of the wings, each featuring visible exhaust nozzles. The tail section features a distinctive twin vertical stabilizer in an H-tail configuration, providing stability and control. The nose is pointed and houses a forward-placed, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy and visible landing gear beneath. The surface is primarily metallic gray with subtle panel lines and some yellow accent markings. The overall appearance suggests a high-performance, possibly military or experimental aircraft designed for speed and agility.
description: A sleek, angular airplane with swept mid-wings, twin rear jet engines, and a twin vertical stabilizer H-tail. The metallic gray body features a forward cockpit and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit, and the wide, swept wings with jet engines at the rear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the angular fuselage, swept wings, and the exposed landing gear beneath the nose.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin jet engines, H-tail configuration, and the broad wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the angular body, swept wings, and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, sharply swept wings with moderate anhedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted at the rear of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units, partially extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single clear canopy, likely single or dual seat.
